videos.title: New Audi Q7 just DESTROYED BMW
videos.summary_source_text:
New Audi Q7 just DESTROYED BMW The all-new Audi Q7 has been revealed, and we’re going to explore the exterior and interior changes, coupled with the back seat space, the engine choices, and the price. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-04-07

This is the all-new Audi Q7! 

Audi’s competitor to the BMW X7 and Mercedes GLS has had a full design refresh, and Mat’s flown over to Germany to see what’s changed. So what exactly is new? Let’s get into it… 

Starting with the exterior design, it’s fair to say that the new Q7 certainly stands out. Up front there’s a huge, illuminated grille, and this is flanked by a pair of OLED headlights. It’s also worth noting that the headlights are absolutely fantastic - there are several different light signatures, and the full beams can act as a projector when you approach/walk away from the car! Along the side, the Q7 comes with up to 23-inch alloy wheels, and the rear pillar of the car has been lifted to allow more space for people in the third row. Around the back, there are OLED taillights, a full-length light bar, and (finally!) the return of real exhaust tips! 

The inside of the Q7 is also super smart, especially with the wood trim that we have in this car. There’s a huge curved dial and infotainment screen, although as in other recent Audis, it has an insanely large bezel around it! Still though, the cabin is well laid out, and it’s (mostly) covered in soft touch materials. There’s lots of storage, the seats are incredibly comfortable, and the new drive selector stalks are a great touch. 

The Q7 currently comes with a 3-litre V6 diesel that can produce 245hp or 300hp. It comes with mild hybrid tech to allow for some driving on electric power alone, and both petrol and plug-in hybrid options are due to be available as well. 

The new Q7 will start from around £75,000 when it goes on sale. But do these all-new looks and the incredible light technology offer enough to sway you away from BMW or Mercedes? Stick with Mat to see for yourself!

Mat’s configured a second-hand Q7: https://bit.ly/Mats-Choice-0407

This is the all-new Audi Q7! 

Audi’s competitor to the BMW X7 and Mercedes GLS has had a full design refresh, and Mat’s flown over to Germany to see what’s changed. So what exactly is new? Let’s get into it… 

Starting with the exterior design, it’s fair to say that the new Q7 certainly stands out. Up front there’s a huge, illuminated grille, and this is flanked by a pair of OLED headlights. It’s also worth noting that the headlights are absolutely fantastic - there are several different light signatures, and the full beams can act as a projector when you approach/walk away from the car! Along the side, the Q7 comes with up to 23-inch alloy wheels, and the rear pillar of the car has been lifted to allow more space for people in the third row. Around the back, there are OLED taillights, a full-length light bar, and (finally!) the return of real exhaust tips! 

The inside of the Q7 is also super smart, especially with the wood trim that we have in this car. There’s a huge curved dial and infotainment screen, although as in other recent Audis, it has an insanely large bezel around it! Still though, the cabin is well laid out, and it’s (mostly) covered in soft touch materials. There’s lots of storage, the seats are incredibly comfortable, and the new drive selector stalks are a great touch. 

The Q7 currently comes with a 3-litre V6 diesel that can produce 245hp or 300hp. It comes with mild hybrid tech to allow for some driving on electric power alone, and both petrol and plug-in hybrid options are due to be available as well. 

The new Q7 will start from around £75,000 when it goes on sale. But do these all-new looks and the incredible light technology offer enough to sway you away from BMW or Mercedes? Stick with Mat to see for yourself!

videos.title: How To Hit Your Irons Straighter In Golf
videos.summary_source_text: How To Hit Your Irons Straighter In Golf Your wrist angles are one of the biggest influencers in how straight (or not) you hit your golf shots. In this video we take a look at the mistakes amateur golfers make in their golf swings, and how to correct them in order to hit your iron shots straighter and more consistently. videos.title: BETTING ON RED! Carolina Hurricanes Seeking to Even Stanley Cup Final in Game 4
videos.summary_source_text:
BETTING ON RED! Carolina Hurricanes Seeking to Even Stanley Cup Final in Game 4 It's a must-win Game 4 for the Carolina Hurricanes as they seek to avoid going 3-1 in the Stanley Cup Final against the Vegas Golden Knights. They have not lost back-to-back games in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs; does that streak stay alive on Tuesday night? What are the main storylines heading into Game 4 as the Hurricanes hope to split on the road in Las Vegas before returning home for Game 5 on Thursday? On the Tuesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the first of two main storylines heading into Game 4: the lines from practice on Monday. It seems Rod Brind'Amour is going to keep the lines blended up instead of going back to the regular lines that we've seen for most of the playoffs. Will starting with those lines be easier instead of going to them later on? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on the second main storyline: who starts in the net for Game 4? Brind'Amour stated that they made their decision, but will hold onto it until the game begins. Who do we think it is, and why play the cloak-and-dagger on the goalie for Game 4? Finally, Zach shares his players to watch, Canes to Click, and some keys to the game. Who will score for the Hurricanes in Game 4 and who should we hope to see step up? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: 08.06.26 - Rift Walk-through Including NEW Smart Money Feature with Joe
videos.summary_source_text:
08.06.26 - Rift Walk-through Including NEW Smart Money Feature with Joe 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

videos.title: Joe Sakic Set to Meet the Media Thursday. What Can We Expect?
videos.summary_source_text:
Joe Sakic Set to Meet the Media Thursday. What Can We Expect? Colorado Avalanche face pivotal offseason changes as Joe Sakic prepares to address the media amid speculation about coaching shakeups—will Dave Hakstol be the fall guy after a disappointing power play and playoff exit?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson dissect Avalanche front office moves, Chris MacFarland's departure to Nashville, and the ongoing debate over Hakstol’s future behind the bench. The spotlight turns to the looming contracts of stars like Cale Makar and intriguing free agent projections for defensemen Nick Blankenburg and Brett Kulak. Plus, the hosts grade Jack Drury’s breakout season and test their hockey acumen with a challenging 82–0 Cap Wages roster-building game, building dream lineups from Avalanche history.

Will Sakic’s loyalty keep familiar faces around, or are bold changes coming to revitalize Colorado's Stanley Cup hopes?

videos.title: Reaction to the Brendan Sorsby Ruling & Its IMPACT on College Football
videos.summary_source_text:
Reaction to the Brendan Sorsby Ruling & Its IMPACT on College Football DNVR Buffs reacts to the controversial Brendan Sorsby ruling after a Texas judge cleared the Texas Tech quarterback to play in 2026 despite the NCAA’s gambling eligibility decision. The crew breaks down what this means for college football, the Big 12, Texas Tech, Colorado Buffaloes football, NCAA authority, sports betting rules, and the future of competitive integrity. Is this the moment the NCAA loses control for good, and could this ruling change how college football handles eligibility, NIL money, gambling violations, and court involvement forever?

videos.title: Skating Mechanics That Help You Catch Passes Softly
videos.summary_source_text:
Skating Mechanics That Help You Catch Passes Softly The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Should the Denver Nuggets Take An “Evaluation” Year? | DNVR Nuggets Podcast Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Should the Denver Nuggets Take An “Evaluation” Year? | DNVR Nuggets Podcast Live The Denver Nuggets don’t have an obvious answer for their roster construction issues heading into this offseason. Nikola Jokic is the only obvious mainstay but every other roster spot feels up for grabs and the team could go several directions. Run it back with or without Peyton Watson and Cam Johnson? Trade Jamal Murray for another star? Is it even possible to move off Aaron Gordon and Christian Braun? The DNVR Nuggets podcast team looks at Jake Coyne’s annual offseason manifesto.

videos.title: Shopping Online Is About To Change Forever
videos.summary_source_text:
Shopping Online Is About To Change Forever #Ad You’re going to be hearing about this AI trend a lot soon: agentic commerce.

Basically, instead of searching through endless websites trying to figure out what to buy, AI agents start doing the discovery work for you before you even search so you’re matched to the right products with less work. 

One leader in the agentic commerce space is called @glanceinspires and I used it to match me with new clothing pieces based on my style, hair color, skin tone, body shape, even the weather. 

But this is way bigger than just fashion. I can see this being used for travel, home decor, cars, tech… basically all of e-commerce is going to be shifting in this direction. 

And I’m all about using AI to make everyday tasks like shopping easier. 

So if you’re on the same boat, check out what Glance is building at the link in the comments. #Ad You’re going to be hearing about this AI trend a lot soon: agentic commerce.

videos.title: Why Does He Look Like He’s Going To Fall?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Does He Look Like He’s Going To Fall? Connor McDavid leans so far into his turns it looks like he's about to fall down, and most people write it off as freakish balance. It isn't. That lean is the engine behind the best crossovers in the world, and it's far more trainable than it looks.

When McDavid tips into a turn he isn't fighting to stay upright. He's falling into the turn on purpose and letting gravity pull him around faster than any push could. Most skaters never tap into it because they lean the wrong way, forward instead of out to the side, so they spend the whole turn working against gravity and not using centrifugal force.
 
That's the gap between the NHL's best and everyone else. Here's an honest test next time you're on the ice. When you turn, are you leaning forward into the circle or out to the side like Connor McDavid?

videos.title: THE #1 VEGETABLE FOR YOUR HEART, ARTERIES & BLOOD FLOW | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
THE #1 VEGETABLE FOR YOUR HEART, ARTERIES & BLOOD FLOW | Dr. Mandell Could one simple vegetable help support your heart, blood vessels, circulation, and overall cardiovascular health? In this video, Dr. Mandell explains why beets are one of his favorite vegetables for supporting healthy blood flow and vascular function. Learn how naturally occurring nitrates in beets can be converted into nitric oxide, a powerful molecule that helps blood vessels relax and supports healthy circulation throughout the body. We'll discuss the connection between nitric oxide, endothelial function, oxygen delivery, exercise performance, healthy aging, and cardiovascular wellness. You'll also discover simple ways to add beets into your diet, including roasted beets, salads, beet juice, and beet powder. Small daily habits can make a big difference over time, and supporting your circulation is one of the most important investments you can make for long-term health.

videos.title: ChatGPT Atlas Explained: The Biggest Browser Shift Since Chrome?
videos.summary_source_text:
ChatGPT Atlas Explained: The Biggest Browser Shift Since Chrome? Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
You've clicked through tabs and typed searches manually your whole life online. In October 2025, OpenAI quietly launched something that rewrites that — ChatGPT Atlas, a full browser built around AI instead of a chatbot bolted onto one.

In this breakdown I walk through exactly what Atlas is, where it came from, what its agentic mode and memory features actually do, how it stacks up against Chrome and Safari, and — the part the promos skip — the real privacy trade-offs you should weigh before handing your browser to an AI. No hype, no fake benchmarks. Just what it does, what it costs you, and whether it's worth switching.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 — Intro
01:00 — The Origin Story   ⚠️ replace with real timestamp
02:33 — The Features That Change Everything
04:09 — The Competitive Landscape
05:33 — The Trade-Offs You Need to Know
06:57 — The Verdict

🔍 What you'll learn:
- How ChatGPT → ChatGPT Search → Atlas was a deliberate runway, not a surprise
- What "agentic mode" actually means (it acts, it doesn't just suggest)
- Why the memory feature may matter more than the automation
- Why Atlas isn't trying to beat Chrome — it's trying to be a different category
- The privacy tension OpenAI itself is being unusually candid about

If this helped, tell me in the comments: switching to Atlas, or staying on Chrome? Genuinely want to know where you're landing. Subscribe for more evidence-first breakdowns of what's actually happening in AI.

videos.title: ‘Fearless, Peerless’ Kimi & ‘Bamboozled’ Russell | Monaco GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
‘Fearless, Peerless’ Kimi & ‘Bamboozled’ Russell | Monaco GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast Tom Clarkson is joined in the Monte Carlo paddock by F1TV lead presenter Laura Winter and F1 correspondent Lawrence Barretto to dissect a dramatic Monaco Grand Prix.

Kimi Antonelli secured his fifth win in a row, extending his championship lead to 66 points. What did we learn about Kimi this weekend? And is he proving to be a ‘generational talent’?

On the other side of the Mercedes garage, a drive-through penalty left George Russell out of the points. He says he’s ‘bamboozled’ by his recent struggles. So what’s going wrong for George? Where does he go from here? And is he fighting for more than just the title now?

Ferrari also endured mixed fortunes in Monaco. Lewis Hamilton scored his second P2 in a row, while Charles Leclerc crashed out late in the race. The guys discuss why Lewis is starting to gain momentum in the team and the ‘emotional rollercoaster’ Charles is currently riding, after announcing a new multi-year deal with Ferrari.

Also on the agenda, Isack Hadjar’s first podium for Red Bull, Max Verstappen’s retirement on lap 1 and more reliability concerns for McLaren.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Get GREAT at Pullups (GUARANTEED!)
videos.summary_source_text:
The Fastest Way to Get GREAT at Pullups (GUARANTEED!) If you can't do many pullups right now and you're looking for a way to more pull-ups, you've come to right place. In this video, I am going to give you a well-designed progression for beginners to increase their pullups by up to 44%. While using isometrics isn't the normally prescribed progression to do more pull-ups, they might be the secret to unlocking greater gains when you are looking to do more pullups quickly.

Do More Pull-Ups Here: https://athleanx.com/morepullups
Subscribe to This Channel Here: http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

Since you are looking for how to do more pullups, this progression I show you in the video is going to do just that. By using isometrics, you are tapping into strength you likely didn't know you had. Isometrics are great way to build strength at different points in the range of motion of the pullup. As a matter of fact, you are stronger isometrically than you are concentrically. Thus, strengthening the different parts of the range of motion will help you get past the most common sticking points on the pull-up.

It starts with the first isometric hold where you are keeping your chin over the bar. To get here, use a chair to step up to the pullup bar so that you are not wasting effort or strength. Try to hold this as long as possible, at least 5 seconds. Once your strength gives out and you can't hold the position any longer - you don't want to let yourself just drop down. Instead, look to lower yourself slowly as this will help build eccentric strength as well, the strongest phase of the range of motion.

Once you finish that rep, step up to the pull-up bar with the top of your head just under the bar. You are again going to hold this position for as long as you can and slowly lower yourself down as you begin to fail. Remember, if you build your isometric and eccentric strength, it will have a great carryover to the concentric portion of the exercise (where you pull yourself up to the pullup bar). Your strength building via isometrics at this point is important for combating the sticking point of the range of motion.

Next, you are going to step up to the pull-up bar but keep yourself in the mid-range of the exercise. Working on the isometric strength here, once again, fights the sticking point. Remember to lower yourself slowly, fighting the negative.

Finally, at once of the hardest points of the range of motion - the initial pull to get yourself going - you are going to depress your scapulae and pull yourself up just enough to get your head to the level of your biceps while keeping them away from your ears. To help with this, give a slight lean back. You're going to fight the negative as hard as you can, just like on every other rep.

If you find yourself crashing towards the floor at any point of the pullup isometric holds, simply unweight your body slightly by stepping onto a resistance band that is looped around the pull-up bar.

The next time you perform this workout, you are going to start from the bottom of the range of motion and go backwards so that you are ending with your head above the pullup bar. That means you are working strength at the bottom of the exercise while you are freshest.

Perform this pullup progression 3-4 times per week, alternating the pull-up progression each time you do it. 

If you can do 10 or more pullups, you're progression is better suited to be volume based. In this scenario to do more pull-ups, you're performing more pull-ups to accrue more exercise volume to get stronger rather than using isometrics.

If you are looking to do more pullups with the volume based approach, simply head over to athleanx.com/domorepullups to get your free 30 day plan to do more pull-ups.

If you are looking for a complete workout program that helps you not only do more pullups, but to get stronger, build muscle, and get more athletic, head to athleanx.com using the link below and check out the program selector to find the best program for your goals.

For more videos on how to do more pullups and get stronger, be sure to subscribe to our YouTube channel using the link below and turn on notifications so you never miss a new video when it’s published. videos.title: The Biggest Microsoft Build News in 82 seconds
videos.summary_source_text:
The Biggest Microsoft Build News in 82 seconds Did @Microsoft actually take the lead in the AI race?

I just got back from Microsoft Build last week, and they dropped A TON of news, but here's what's actually important. 

They released 7 NEW in-house Microsoft AI models that focus on reasoning, coding, images, and voice. 

Plus they're making a big bet on AI Agents with the new Microsoft Scout. It's Microsoft’s new "Autopilot" agent powered by OpenClaw that has direct access to literally manage Windows for you. It can operate across Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint to handle your chats, emails, and calendar.

Even with all of these new releases, it's still TBD which AI products will get the most usage and outlast the others. Let me know who you think is winning in the comments 🤔

videos.title: I Almost Walked Off The Course... (The Hardest Golf Round of My Life)
videos.summary_source_text:
I Almost Walked Off The Course... (The Hardest Golf Round of My Life) The team at the Golf Travel Centre sent me to West Cliffs and Praia D'El Rey to put my game to the absolute test. And wow... it almost broke me. 

After a nightmare front nine where I was completely lost in my own head, dealing with a terrible mental scorecard, and hitting awful iron shots, I seriously contemplated walking off the course and heading straight back to the clubhouse. But giving up isn't an option. 

Stick around for the back nine recovery, where things completely turn around, including what might be one of the best up-and-down par saves of my entire life! Stick around until the end, where I break down my actual Arccos golf data stats to see exactly how many shots I lost to a tour pro. 

Can I fix my approach, play and short game in time to try and qualify for senior tour events? Let me know in the comments where I should play next!

0:00 - The Ultimate Golf Challenge at West Cliffs
1:12 - Total Nightmare Start (Losing My Mental Game)
2:35 - Facing Disaster: The Mental Scorecard Fail
3:54 - The Best Up and Down of My Life!
5:10 - Struggling with Hanging Lies & Deep Bunkers
6:48 - Front Nine Recap: I Want to Quit
7:59 - The Back Nine Turnaround: COME ON!
9:15 - Clutch Putts and Finding the Fairway

videos.title: Driver Made Simple Part 5 — Squaring the Face
videos.summary_source_text:
Driver Made Simple Part 5 — Squaring the Face Here's the move the pros use to hit a draw and compress the ball: early face squaring from the top. 🎯 As you start your downswing, bow that left wrist so the palm faces your chest. Right hand knuckles point back. That squares the face before impact — not after — and that's the difference between a flush strike and a slice. 

Get your own Blue Brick https://sbrew.link/9LDJftLE
And Hit Your Drives Long and Straight

videos.title: Every Shot From Eugenio Chacarra's Winning Back Nine | KLM Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Every Shot From Eugenio Chacarra's Winning Back Nine | KLM Open Rewatch Eugenio Chacarra’s winning Back 9 at the KLM Open in the Back 9 on Sunday presented by Nexo. The Spaniard birdied the 18th hole to secure a one-shot victory, claiming his second DP World Tour title in his 45th start and moving up to sixth in the Race to Dubai Rankings.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The European Swing continues as the DP World Tour hosts a national open for the third consecutive week. Connor Syme returns to defend his maiden DP world Tour title where he won by two shots 12 months ago. Alongside Syme, Major Winners Francesco Molinari and Danny Willet will be teeing it up as well as Casey Jarvis and Joost Luiten who will be bidding for a hat-trick of wins at his home open. It will also be a fond farewell to The International which is hosting the KLM Open for the third consecutive year and fourth time overall and has always been a jewel in the crown of the DP World Tour Schedule. It is also the sight where Beat the Pro launched back in 2016 and has produced many memorable moments throughout the years.
Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: Skatterna och Valet - Sverigedemokraternas Oscar Sjöstedt
videos.summary_source_text:
Skatterna och Valet - Sverigedemokraternas Oscar Sjöstedt Inför höstens riksdagsval intervjuar våra skatteexperter Oscar Warglo och Ulrika Lundh Eriksson representanter från samtliga riksdagspartier om deras syn på skatter. 

Denna gång möter Oscar Warglo Sverigedemokraternas ekonomiskpolitiska talesperson Oscar Sjöstedt som bland annat berättar om partiets inriktning på att kompensera hushållen efter höginflationsperioden. 

Oscar Sjöstedt lyfter bl a fram följande:  
 
Sänkta hushållsrelaterade skatter såsom inkomstskatt och elskatt  
Högsta marginalskatt max 50% - löntagare ska ha hälften kvar  
Nej till fastighetsskatt, arvs- och gåvoskatt samt förmögenhetsskatt  
Nej till beredskapsskatt  
 

Sjöstedt betonar att en övergripande skattereform inte är realistisk eftersom partierna står för långt ifrån varandra. SD vill fokusera på hushållen som tog smällen under höginflationsperioden och göra tuffare prioriteringar på utgiftssidan. 
 
Vill du veta mer om hur de olika riksdagspartierna ser på framtidens skattepolitik? Besök vår webbsida "Skatterna och valet 2026 https://www.pwc.se/skatterna-och-valet

Sidan uppdateras löpande med nya intervjuer och annan information relaterad till skatter och valet.

videos.title: LOW HANDS | Your Hands Are The Secret To A GREAT Golf Swing
videos.summary_source_text:
LOW HANDS | Your Hands Are The Secret To A GREAT Golf Swing In this video, Eric Cogorno demonstrates how to keep the hands low in the golf swing during the takeaway and downswing for incredible benefits! This simple technique helps you stay in posture in golf swing, keeps your left arm straight, and delivers better ball striking like PGA Tour Pros Nelly Korda and Adam Scott.

 ⏱️ Timestamps: 
🔍0:31 - "Hands low, club up" mantra for takeaway and downswing 
📐 1:08 - How keeping your hands low will help you stay in posture in the golf swing 
❌ 1:38 - High hands mistakes that ruin ball striking 
🎯 2:12 – PGA Tour examples - Nelly Korda and Adam Scott  
📏 2:40 - Belt line checkpoint for proper hand positioning 
💪 3:10 - Left shoulder down during the takeaway 
🔄 4:14 – How to keep the hands low through impact 
✅ 5:19 – Wrist hinge 
🏆 7:38 – Maintaining posture and straight arms "for free"

Keeping your hands low in the golf swing helps you stay in posture and keep your lead arm straighter. Watch how PGA Tour Pros Nelly Korda and Adam Scott execute perfect takeaway mechanics keeping their hands below the belt line and the club up. This method will help you to stop standing up in the golf swing and help you  play better golf with more consistent contact.

What You'll Learn:
• The "hands low, club up" mantra that transformed a student's ball striking
• Why keeping the hands low in golf swing helps golfers maintain proper golf posture
• How PGA Tour Pros Nelly Korda and Adam Scott position their hands during the takeaway and downswing
• How to keep the left shoulder down for consistent ball striking
• How to stay in posture in golf swing without thinking about it
• How to stop standing up in the golf swing by keeping the hands low in the golf swing
• Why low hands keep your lead arm straighter automatically
• Simple adjustments that help you how to play better golf

videos.title: Cancer Dies When You Do This
videos.summary_source_text:
Cancer Dies When You Do This What would you do if you were diagnosed with cancer?

In this video, Dr. Berg explains the first strategy he would focus on, and it’s not asking which drug to take. Cancer cells are extremely fragile and heavily dependent on constant fuel and resources to survive. Instead of only focusing on destroying cancer cells, this approach focuses on changing the environment they rely on.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out:  https://drbrg.co/4xedR9J 

Discover how prolonged fasting may create conditions that cancer cells struggle to survive in, while healthy cells adapt and thrive. Learn the science behind metabolic health, cellular stress, and why changing the body’s environment could play a major role in supporting overall health.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/3PrrZLV

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

What would really happen if you ate 4 eggs every single day?

In this video, we break down the real science behind eggs and what they actually do for your body.

Eggs are often called a complete superfood for a reason; they provide high-quality protein, essential fats, vitamins, and critical nutrients your body needs to function.

The BRAND NEW Wilson putters are...🤯 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: HEARTBREAK! Carolina Hurricanes Lose 5-4 in Double Overtime Thriller After 4-Goal Comeback
videos.summary_source_text:
HEARTBREAK! Carolina Hurricanes Lose 5-4 in Double Overtime Thriller After 4-Goal Comeback This Stanley Cup Final has had it all through three games. Multiple lead changes, multiple comebacks, goals overturned, and so much more. Game 3 was no exception, as the Hurricanes erased a four-goal deficit to go from down 4-0 to tie it 4-4 and force overtime. However, they would lose in double overtime, 5-4, after a bad bounce went off Brandon Bussi and into the net. Despite the ending, how much of an instant classic did Game 3 become? Also, what stood out in the loss that indicates that the Hurricanes can bounce back in Game 4? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on Game 3 as a whole. Between two goals getting overturned, a four-goal comeback, and an overtime thriller, Game 3 was pure cinema. How did Zach feel about the Hurricanes having to make another comeback? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how the team did not do Frederik Andersen any favors, seeing him get pulled after the second period. Did he give up goals he wanted back? Yes. Did the team leave him to dry? Yes. Also, how huge was Brandon Bussi to keep them in the game and force it into a double overtime? Finally, Zach shares his thoughts on what this game could mean for Game 4 on Tuesday. What could the goalie situation look like? If William Carrier can't play, who draws in for him? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Avalanche Will Make Changes, But Don't Expect Bold Moves This Offseason
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche Will Make Changes, But Don't Expect Bold Moves This Offseason Colorado Avalanche fans brace for a quiet offseason—are major shakeups off the table after a rollercoaster year? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson address whether the Avalanche will “run it back” or make bold moves, weighing potential trades involving Valeri Nichushkin and Ross Colton. With Cale Makar’s contract expected to be the summer's headline, the hosts explore if depth tweaks are enough for a return to playoff dominance, or if targeting a top-six forward like Dylan Larkin even makes sense within the team’s cap constraints.

The episode spotlights Ross Colton’s production drop, his future in Denver, and grades his season performance, while dissecting the team’s strategy after another tough postseason exit. NHL award buzz heats up as Scott Wedgewood’s Vezina Trophy finish, surprising voting results, and snubs for standout goaltenders like Jake Oettinger and Connor Hellebuyck round out a candid Avalanche-centric post-mortem. Will Colorado’s roster stability lead to redemption or stagnation next year?

videos.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush
videos.summary_source_text:
Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush Prompt engineering may no longer be the real advantage in AI. A new idea called harness engineering is starting to take over, and it explains why the same model can become far more useful depending on the system built around it. Instead of only changing prompts, companies are now focusing on tools, memory, context, permissions, verification, recovery paths, and feedback loops that make AI agents work reliably over time.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

📌 What You’ll See:
Microsoft Research paper on Retrospective Harness Optimization for AI agents
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05922
Microsoft Work IQ and the new context layer for AI agents
SOURCE: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/blog/2026/06/02/announcing-the-new-work-iq-apis/
Meta AI chatbot breach showing why agent safeguards matter
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/legal/government/high-profile-meta-ai-chatbot-breach-spotlights-security-risks-automation-2026-06-03/
AI Harness Engineering as a runtime system for software agents
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.13357

🚨 Why It Matters
Harness engineering could become one of the biggest advantages in the next phase of AI. As models become more similar, the real difference may come from the system around them: the tools, memory, checks, context, permissions, and workflows that turn raw intelligence into reliable work.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 and Playoff | the Memorial | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 and Playoff | the Memorial | 2026 Check out all the best shots from the final round of the Memorial, featuring Wyndham Clark, J.T. Poston, Sam Burns and Ryan Gerard, among others. 
 
The PGA TOUR returns to one of its most prestigious stops this week with the Memorial Tournament presented by Workday at Muirfield Village Golf Club, where a world-class field assembles for the latest Signature Event of the 2026 season. Headlining the field is World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, who arrives seeking a rare piece of PGA TOUR history as he attempts to become the first player since Steve Stricker (2009-11 John Deere Classic) to win the same tournament in three consecutive years. Scheffler, who finished solo third in his most recent start at THE CJ CUP Byron Nelson, has thrived at Jack Nicklaus’ famed layout and returns as the two-time defending champion. Rory McIlroy makes his first appearance at Muirfield Village since 2024, bringing renewed momentum after his Masters victory earlier this season. The four-time major champion recorded four consecutive top-20 finishes at the Memorial before missing last year’s edition, with a career-best result of T4 in 2016. Joining Scheffler and McIlroy is a deep group of contenders, including FedExCup Nos. 2 and 3 Cameron Young and Matt Fitzpatrick, who have combined for five victories already in 2026. Fitzpatrick owns three career top-10 finishes at the Memorial, while Young posted his best result at the event a year ago. Several of the TOUR’s hottest players also arrive in strong form. Ludvig Åberg continues an impressive spring run that has featured six top-eight finishes in his last nine starts, while Russell Henley returns fresh off his victory at the Charles Schwab Challenge and looks to build on last year’s T5 finish at Muirfield Village. The field also welcomes back PGA Championship winner Aaron Rai, who makes his first start since lifting the Wanamaker Trophy. Overall, the field boasts nine of the top 10 players in the Official World Golf Ranking, including Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood, Xander Schauffele and J.J. Spaun, highlighting the elite nature of the Signature Event format. Played on the historic Jack Nicklaus and Desmond Muirhead design, Muirfield Village Golf Club has been a fixture on the PGA TOUR schedule since 1976 and remains one of the circuit’s most demanding tests. With 700 FedExCup points available to the winner and Scheffler chasing a landmark third straight title, the Memorial Tournament once again promises to be one of the defining weeks of the PGA TOUR season.

videos.title: This Is How Tour Pros Get Their Clubs Built!
videos.summary_source_text:
This Is How Tour Pros Get Their Clubs Built! Welcome behind the scenes at The Trott Shop, where every club is built to the same exacting standards we demanded on Tour.

In this video, I'm excited to introduce a new face to the channel—please join me in welcoming Evan Aguirre, affectionately nicknamed by yours truly as Evan 2 Chainz.

Evan brings incredible energy, work ethic, and attention to detail to everything he does. Just as importantly, he understands that every club leaving our shop represents our reputation. Precision, consistency, and quality are non-negotiable if you're delivering a true Tour-level experience.

Watch closely as Evan takes you through our build process, showing exactly what goes into creating clubs that perform at the highest level. If you've ever wondered what separates a Tour-standard build from a retail build, this is your front-row seat.

Take notes, enjoy the process, and drop any questions in the comments section—we'll do our best to answer them all.

videos.title: A Hug Does More Than You Think
videos.summary_source_text:
A Hug Does More Than You Think Most people think of a hug as an emotional experience, but it's also a biological one. Gentle, sustained touch activates pathways in the brain that promote feelings of safety and belonging while simultaneously calming the body's stress response. As vagal tone improves, heart rate slows, blood pressure can come down, and the body shifts into a more restorative state. Human connection may be doing far more for health than we realize.

This is a clip from my video "How One Simple Behavior Lowers Heart Rate, Blood Pressure, and Inflammation"

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.


About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Cardiovascular Interventions, P.A. in Orlando Florida where, since 1990,  he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor performing thousands of interventional procedures in hospital and out patient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease . He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. videos.title: TAKE 1/8 TEASPOON DAILY — YOUR HEART & ARTERIES WILL THANK YOU | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
TAKE 1/8 TEASPOON DAILY — YOUR HEART & ARTERIES WILL THANK YOU | Dr. Mandell Could a tiny pinch of ground cayenne pepper each day help support your heart, arteries, circulation, and overall cardiovascular health? In this video, I’ll show you exactly how I take it and explain the science behind why this powerful spice has gained so much attention. Simply mix a small pinch of cayenne pepper, about 1/8 teaspoon or less, with a few drops of honey or another natural sweetener to help soften the heat and make it more comfortable to take. If you're sensitive to spicy foods, start with an even smaller amount and gradually work your way up. Take it immediately before a meal so the food that follows helps buffer the spice. We'll discuss capsaicin, the active compound in cayenne pepper, and how it supports nitric oxide production, healthy vasodilation, blood vessel relaxation, circulation, oxygen delivery, nutrient transport, endothelial function, and healthy blood pressure already within a normal range. Your arteries and blood vessels work around the clock to deliver oxygen and nutrients to every organ, tissue, and cell in your body. Supporting this incredible vascular system is one of the most important things you can do for long-term health, energy, vitality, and longevity. Watch until the end as I explain exactly what happens inside the body after consuming this tiny daily amount and why so many people are adding cayenne pepper to their wellness routine. As always, consistency is where the real benefits occur, and small daily habits practiced over time can have a profound impact on overall health.

*********************************

videos.title: Dö med noll på kontot: hiss eller diss?
videos.summary_source_text:
Dö med noll på kontot: hiss eller diss? Bill Perkins bok "Die with Zero" ifrågasätter om vi sparar för mycket och upplever för lite. Tanken att front-loada upplevelserna till de år i livet när vi faktiskt kan njuta av dem är genuint intressant och stämmer med idén om att ge med varm hand snarare än kall.

Men att matematiskt sikta på noll är riskabelt. Du vet inte hur länge du lever eller vad vård på äldre dagar kostar.

Min dom: hiss på tankesättet, diss på att noll ska vara det faktiska målet.

Mer om pension, arv och att planera för livets sista kapitel finns på rikatillsammans.se och i RikaTillsammans-podden.

videos.title: How Big Tech Lies About AI Layoffs
videos.summary_source_text:
How Big Tech Lies About AI Layoffs 🚨 The "AI is taking our jobs" narrative is apparently a massive corporate cover-up.

Big companies aren’t laying off thousands of people because AI suddenly replaced them overnight. They’re laying people off because they over-hired and got WAY too bloated. 📉

Instead of CEOs admitting they made a massive management mistake (which looks terrible to investors), they’re saying "we're becoming more efficient with AI" instead and hoping it'll pump stock prices.

But it seems like investors have caught on. Companies using the "AI excuse" are actually seeing their stock prices drop nowadays.

What do you think? Are companies using AI to hide bad management, or is AI a real threat in your industry?

#AI #AInews #Layoffs #BusinessNews 🚨 The "AI is taking our jobs" narrative is apparently a massive corporate cover-up.

Big companies aren’t laying off thousands of people because AI suddenly replaced them overnight. They’re laying people off because they over-hired and got WAY too bloated. 📉

Instead of CEOs admitting they made a massive management mistake (which looks terrible to investors), they’re saying "we're becoming more efficient with AI" instead and hoping it'll pump stock prices.

But it seems like investors have caught on. Companies using the "AI excuse" are actually seeing their stock prices drop nowadays.

What do you think? Are companies using AI to hide bad management, or is AI a real threat in your industry?

videos.title: Final Round Highlights | 2026 KLM Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Final Round Highlights | 2026 KLM Open The final round of the KLM Open at The International in Amsterdam proved to be a true test from start to finish, with tough winds making scoring incredibly difficult on Sunday. Players struggled to hold onto momentum as shots were dropped throughout the day, with the challenging conditions creating drama across the leaderboard right until the closing holes. But while others battled to stay level, Eugenio Chacarra rose to the occasion, handling the pressure and the windy conditions brilliantly to come out on top and secure victory in the Netherlands.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The European Swing continues as the DP World Tour hosts a national open for the third consecutive week. Connor Syme returns to defend his maiden DP world Tour title where he won by two shots 12 months ago. Alongside Syme, Major Winners Francesco Molinari and Danny Willet will be teeing it up as well as Casey Jarvis and Joost Luiten who will be bidding for a hat-trick of wins at his home open. It will also be a fond farewell to The International which is hosting the KLM Open for the third consecutive year and fourth time overall and has always been a jewel in the crown of the DP World Tour Schedule. It is also the sight where Beat the Pro launched back in 2016 and has produced many memorable moments throughout the years.
Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: Takomo 101 MKII Irons Tested at Average Swing Speeds
videos.summary_source_text:
Takomo 101 MKII Irons Tested at Average Swing Speeds How does the Takomo 101 MKII perform at the swing speeds most golfers actually have?

In this test, Takomo Tim puts the 101 MKII iron through its paces at three different swing speeds: 70 mph (GW), 75 mph (8 Iron), and 80 mph (5 Iron). We compare carry distance, ball speed, launch, spin, descent angle, and consistency to see how the club performs for everyday golfers—not just high-speed players.

If you're considering the Takomo 101 MKII and want to know how it performs at realistic swing speeds, this video provides the numbers you need to make an informed decision.

videos.title: How to Skate like The Fastest Players in the NHL (feat. Nathan MacKinnon)
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Skate like The Fastest Players in the NHL (feat. Nathan MacKinnon) How to Skate like The Fastest Players in the NHL (feat. Nathan MacKinnon)

The fastest player with Connor McDavid in the NHL right now arguably is Nathan MacKinnon and the thing that makes his stride so devastating is that he isn't really pushing the way most coaches teach it. Downhill skating comes down to rotation and letting gravity do the work, and once you see what he's actually doing with his ankles, knees, and blade pressure, you'll change your opinion on how the stride is being taught by traditional powerskating measure. 

Inside the video we go through the 3 biomechanical keys that make MacKinnon's forward stride so devastating, plus the on-ice and off-ice drills you can use to build each one into your own skating. 

👉 Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: This PROVES Golf Clubs are OVER PRICED!
videos.summary_source_text:
This PROVES Golf Clubs are OVER PRICED! Can a driver that costs just £250 really compete with the big names in golf? Guy puts the new Benross RS Driver through a full H.I.T review, testing its performance, forgiveness, distance and value to find out whether this could be the best budget driver on the market. If you're thinking about upgrading your driver without spending a fortune, this is one review you won't want to miss.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Why Are The Best Skaters Always Falling?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Are The Best Skaters Always Falling? Want to skate with that deep, effortless lean where it looks like you're defying gravity around every turn? First thing you want to change is your mindset to stop leaning and start falling. You want to feel like you’re falling toward the center of the circle with every step. 

It also helps to reach wide, so Imagine planting your step as far from the center as possible reaching all the way to the boards with each step. It will also help to land your inside foot (outside edge) right next to your outside foot (inside edge) for better center of mass displacement. 

These adjustments help you create the deep lean, powerful crossovers, and flow that are part of what make someone like McDavid one of the fastest, most agile players on the planet.

The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

Driver Made Simple Part 4 — Open Your Chest Want more club head speed without swinging harder? It's all in your shoulders. 💪 Try the lawnmower drill: tilt forward into your golf posture and pull an imaginary cord back toward your chest, then extend through to the other side. Retract on the backswing, protract on the follow-through. This move alone can add serious yards to your drives. 

Get your own Blue Brick https://sbrew.link/GvIFZMkZ
And Hit Your Drives Long and Straight

videos.title: Fatty Liver Fix (Do This for 6 Days)
videos.summary_source_text:
Fatty Liver Fix (Do This for 6 Days) This fatty liver fix can reduce liver fat and improve liver health in as little as 6 days. Discover 4 evidence-based ways to reverse a fatty liver naturally, improve liver function, and support your overall health. 

0:00 Introduction: Fatty liver fix, fast!
1:01 What causes a fatty liver?
3:00 Fatty liver symptoms
4:53 Waist size and liver health 
6:27 Research on how to reverse a fatty liver
7:28 Coffee and fatty liver disease
8:36 How to reverse a fatty liver

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4dR3cKz

Discover the best and worst foods for your liver. Watch these next:
▶️ https://youtu.be/F84ijY7Iofg
▶️ https://youtu.be/lo77w0bP4Qs

A low-carb diet is one of the best ways to reverse a fatty liver. Read more here: https://drbrg.co/4g3koxK

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Belly fat is one of the most common symptoms of a fatty liver. This excess abdominal fat is often caused by visceral fat, which is closely linked to poor liver health. Simply focusing on weight loss is not always the best way to fix a fatty liver.

A fatty liver is not caused by eating fat. It’s typically caused by fructose consumption. Fructose can only be metabolized by the liver, where it is converted into liver fat, contributing to inflammation and liver damage.

Common fatty liver symptoms include:
 • Darkened skin on the back of the neck or groin area
 • Skin tags
 • A protruding belly

Waist size is often a key indicator of liver health. Measure your baseline waist circumference before implementing this fatty liver fix, then measure again 6 days later. Many people notice a significant reduction in waist size in a short period of time.

DATA:
https://www.ucsf.edu/news/2017/08/408151/switching-sugar-starch-leads-less-fatty-liver-kids 
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27824642/ 
https://gut.bmj.com/content/60/Suppl_1/A240.1 
https://pennstatehealthnews.org/2023/02/exercise-as-treatment-for-nonalcoholic-fatty-liver-disease/
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12819859/

videos.title: The #1 BEST Vitamin for Arthritis
videos.summary_source_text:
The #1 BEST Vitamin for Arthritis Arthritis may not just be about aging; calcium buildup in the joints could be a major hidden cause.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/3PRYzqn

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

Many people focus only on pain relief without addressing what may be happening inside the joints. This video explains a different perspective on arthritis and joint health that many people have never heard before.

In this video, Dr. Berg explains how one overlooked vitamin may help remove calcium buildup and support healthier joints naturally.

Vitamin K2 plays a critical role in directing calcium to the right places in the body, like the bones and teeth, while helping prevent calcium from building up in soft tissues and joints. This is why vitamin K2 has become such an important nutrient for joint stiffness, inflammation, and cartilage support.

videos.title: 06.06.26 - Ep.BONUS - Smart Money Revealed: Managed Capital, Mirror Wallets & the Future of Trading
videos.summary_source_text:
06.06.26 - Ep.BONUS - Smart Money Revealed: Managed Capital, Mirror Wallets & the Future of Trading In this special bonus episode of RIFT Before You Quit, we introduce one of the most anticipated additions to the RIFT ecosystem: Smart Money.

This session goes beyond traditional trading education and explores a new participation framework designed around the same principles used by professional capital managers, hedge funds, and institutional investors. Rather than focusing on individual trades, Smart Money focuses on something far more important:

✅ Managed Capital
✅ Managed Risk
✅ Managed Portfolios
✅ Self-Custody

The episode explains why retail traders often struggle despite having access to information, while institutional participants consistently outperform through structure, discipline, and capital management. The core message is simple:

Opportunities are everywhere. Properly managed capital is rare.

You'll discover how RIFT's new Smart Money feature aims to bridge that gap by giving users access to professionally managed trading books while maintaining full ownership and visibility of their funds.

In This Bonus Episode:

🔹 What "Smart Money" really means
🔹 Why institutions focus on risk before opportunity
🔹 The difference between retail trading and professional capital management
🔹 Managed Capital vs Managed Trades
🔹 Portfolio Construction Principles used by professionals
🔹 Capital Preservation and Long-Term Compounding
🔹 How Smart Money introduces institutional concepts to retail participants
🔹 Self-custodial participation and ownership of funds
🔹 Mirror Wallet technology explained
🔹 Capital allocation through managed trading books
🔹 Diversification across multiple trading strategies
🔹 Risk management at the portfolio level
🔹 Capital rotation and opportunity optimization
🔹 Transparency through on-chain infrastructure
🔹 Why Smart Money is different from traditional copy trading
🔹 The evolution from trade copying to capital management infrastructure
🔹 How Smart Money prepares users for future prop trading opportunities
🔹 The roadmap toward professional trader development inside RIFT

One of the most important lessons from this episode is that successful investing is not about predicting every market move. It is about building systems that continuously manage capital, adapt to changing conditions, and preserve risk-adjusted growth over time.

The session also introduces the concept of Mirror Wallets, a unique structure where users maintain custody and transparency while participating alongside managed trading books. This allows participants to follow structured portfolio management principles without surrendering ownership of their capital.

The discussion then expands into broader market realities:

- Why retail traders often become liquidity
- Why information alone isn't enough
- Why infrastructure creates opportunity
- How professional capital flows differently from emotional retail participation
- Why the future of trading is moving toward intelligent infrastructure and managed participation

videos.title: I WASN’T READY FOR THIS! Tuned M140i v Lambo DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
I WASN’T READY FOR THIS! Tuned M140i v Lambo DRAG RACE We’re drag racing a Lamborghini Revuelto against a heavily tuned BMW M140i & Audi S3 over the quarter-mile and half-mile to find out which is the quickest! 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-0606

We’ve got Mat in the Lamborghini Revuelto, and he’s going up against two cars that have been massively tuned by Gravituned Power to see which will come out on top over the quarter-mile! So, let’s get into it… 

Starting with the Lambo, it’s powered by a 6.5-litre NA V12, coupled with three electric motors. These combine to produce 1,015hp and 1,062Nm, with this power sent to all four wheels. It weighs in at 1,872kg, and it’s incredibly expensive, costing £558,000! 

Up next we have the Audi S3. It rolls up with a 3.2-litre turbocharged V6, and this puts down an incredible 950hp and 900Nm! Power is sent to all four wheels via a 7-speed dual-clutch, and it tips the scales at 1,600kg. All in, it’s valued at £70,000. 

Finally we come to the almighty M140. It’s powered by a 3.4-litre 6-cylinder turbo, and in low boost mode this creates 1,200hp and 1,500Nm! This is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed auto, and it’s by far the lightest here, weighing just 1,475kg! The car and the mods combined cost £110,000. 

So how will this one play out - will it be an easy win for the BMW? Or will newer Lambo tech prove to be the winner? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE!

Thanks to everyone who lent us cars for this race:
- BMW: https://www.instagram.com/livinglifefastt/ 
- Audi: https://www.instagram.com/gravituned_power
- Lambo: https://www.instagram.com/seanysgarage/ We’re drag racing a Lamborghini Revuelto against a heavily tuned BMW M140i & Audi S3 over the quarter-mile and half-mile to find out which is the quickest!

Finally we come to the almighty M140. It’s powered by a 3.4-litre 6-cylinder turbo, and in low boost mode this creates 1,200hp and 1,500Nm! This is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed auto, and it’s by far the lightest here, weighing just 1,475kg! The car and the mods combined cost £110,000.

videos.title: Är Dubais bästa burgare värd priset?
videos.summary_source_text:
Är Dubais bästa burgare värd priset? 📖 Köp vår bok: https://www.adlibris.com/sv/bok/burgare-sa-gor-du-varldens-basta-burgare-hemma-9789127191211

Tidigare i år åkte vi till Dubai för att vara domare i Dubai Burger Championship 2026 – Mellanösterns största burgartävling och festival. Eventet samlade några av världens främsta burgarrestauranger som fick skapa exklusiva burgare för en publik på tiotusentals besökare.

I årets upplaga deltog restauranger från Spanien, Brasilien, Argentina, England och Sverige tillsammans med några av Dubais mest populära burgerställen. Burgerdudes satt i juryn och utsåg våra favoriter, samtidigt som vi passade på att utforska stadens burgarscen. Senast vi var i Dubai var 2019, så det hade hänt en hel del.

Här är alla våra recensioner från Dubai:
🍔 https://www.burgerdudes.se/dubai/

🌎 Vi testade burgare från några av världens mest hyllade restauranger, bland annat Burger and Beyond (England), Rekas Burger (Sverige), Holy Burger (Brasilien), La Birra Bar (Argentina) och Hundred Burgers (Spanien). Dessutom provade vi lokala festivalburgare från SALT, Maxzi The Good Food Shop, GOAT Burger, Hella Good, She Burger, One More Burger House, Beau Restaurant, Lokal Bun Burgers, Pickl, MUST, The Meat Avenue och High Joint.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Saturday | the Memorial | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Saturday | the Memorial | 2026 Check out all the best shots from Saturday at the 2026 Memorial, featuring Scottie Scheffler, Rory McIlroy, Wyndham Clark and Sam Burns, among others. Round 3 was suspended on Saturday due to inclement weather and will resume Sunday morning.

The PGA TOUR returns to one of its most prestigious stops this week with the Memorial Tournament presented by Workday at Muirfield Village Golf Club, where a world-class field assembles for the latest Signature Event of the 2026 season. Headlining the field is World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, who arrives seeking a rare piece of PGA TOUR history as he attempts to become the first player since Steve Stricker (2009-11 John Deere Classic) to win the same tournament in three consecutive years. Scheffler, who finished solo third in his most recent start at THE CJ CUP Byron Nelson, has thrived at Jack Nicklaus’ famed layout and returns as the two-time defending champion. Rory McIlroy makes his first appearance at Muirfield Village since 2024, bringing renewed momentum after his Masters victory earlier this season. The four-time major champion recorded four consecutive top-20 finishes at the Memorial before missing last year’s edition, with a career-best result of T4 in 2016. Joining Scheffler and McIlroy is a deep group of contenders, including FedExCup Nos. 2 and 3 Cameron Young and Matt Fitzpatrick, who have combined for five victories already in 2026. Fitzpatrick owns three career top-10 finishes at the Memorial, while Young posted his best result at the event a year ago. Several of the TOUR’s hottest players also arrive in strong form. Ludvig Åberg continues an impressive spring run that has featured six top-eight finishes in his last nine starts, while Russell Henley returns fresh off his victory at the Charles Schwab Challenge and looks to build on last year’s T5 finish at Muirfield Village. The field also welcomes back PGA Championship winner Aaron Rai, who makes his first start since lifting the Wanamaker Trophy. Overall, the field boasts nine of the top 10 players in the Official World Golf Ranking, including Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood, Xander Schauffele and J.J. Spaun, highlighting the elite nature of the Signature Event format. Played on the historic Jack Nicklaus and Desmond Muirhead design, Muirfield Village Golf Club has been a fixture on the PGA TOUR schedule since 1976 and remains one of the circuit’s most demanding tests. With 700 FedExCup points available to the winner and Scheffler chasing a landmark third straight title, the Memorial Tournament once again promises to be one of the defining weeks of the PGA TOUR season.

videos.title: 100 000 kronor på sparkontot: är jag rik?
videos.summary_source_text:
100 000 kronor på sparkontot: är jag rik? Är 100 000 kronor på sparkontot rikt? Relativt sett: ja, de flesta svenska hushåll har mindre i likvida tillgångar. Absolut sett: det täcker tre till fyra månader av en normal hushållsekonomi och ger dig handlingsfrihet, men inte ekonomiskt oberoende.

Jag resonerar om skillnaden mellan att vara rik i andras ögon och vad pengarna faktiskt ger dig för frihet. Och varför den distinktionen är viktigare än beloppet.

Mer om buffert, sparande och ekonomisk frihet finns på rikatillsammans.se och i RikaTillsammans-podden.

videos.title: AI Memory Compared: What ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok & Copilot Actually Remember
videos.summary_source_text:
AI Memory Compared: What ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok & Copilot Actually Remember Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Every AI conversation starts from zero — until now. This is a full breakdown of how persistent memory actually works across ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, Gemini, and Microsoft Copilot: what each one stores, what it ignores, how it handles your privacy, and which one fits which part of your life.

No hype, no fake benchmarks — just what's actually shipped and what the tradeoffs are.

⏱️ Chapters
00:00 Intro — the problem with stateless AI
00:52 What ChatGPT Memory Actually Is Now
01:50 Saved Memories vs Chat History — The Real Difference
03:02 The Memory Summary (This Is the Part That Matters Most)
04:17 How Memory Actually Changes the Answers
05:59 The Privacy Side Nobody Wants to Talk About
07:17 Where This Actually Becomes Useful
08:34 ChatGPT vs Claude: How Memory Differs
09:52 Grok and the Macrohard Wildcard
11:29 Google Gemini and Microsoft Copilot
13:09 Quick Recap Before We Get Practical
14:43 Real-World Scenarios
16:22 The Risks I'd Want You to Take Seriously
17:49 Where This Is Actually Heading

📌 What I'd actually do: pick one system, use it intentionally, and audit what it remembers. Don't let any of them become the default holding tank for your entire context without you choosing it.

videos.title: Round 3 Highlights | 2026 KLM Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Round 3 Highlights | 2026 KLM Open The best bits of the KLM Open at The International in Amsterdam delivered a crucial moving day as the leaderboard began to tighten heading into the final round.

Eugenio Chacarra made his move in round three of the KLM Open at The International in Amsterdam. Winds were still high but it didn't stop major movements on the leaderboard. At the top, the leaderboard couldn’t be split, with two players finishing the day tied for the lead, setting up a dramatic final day.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The European Swing continues as the DP World Tour hosts a national open for the third consecutive week. Connor Syme returns to defend his maiden DP world Tour title where he won by two shots 12 months ago. Alongside Syme, Major Winners Francesco Molinari and Danny Willet will be teeing it up as well as Casey Jarvis and Joost Luiten who will be bidding for a hat-trick of wins at his home open. It will also be a fond farewell to The International which is hosting the KLM Open for the third consecutive year and fourth time overall and has always been a jewel in the crown of the DP World Tour Schedule. It is also the sight where Beat the Pro launched back in 2016 and has produced many memorable moments throughout the years.
Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: The Vagus Nerve and Modern Disease
videos.summary_source_text:
The Vagus Nerve and Modern Disease Most people think stress only affects how they feel, but it also affects how their body functions. The thoughts running through the mind all day long can influence heart rate, blood pressure, breathing patterns, and the balance of the nervous system. In today's world, many people spend their days in a constant state of stimulation, never fully shifting into recovery mode. Creating moments of silence and slowing the breath may be one of the simplest ways to remind the body that it is safe to relax again.


To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

videos.title: Grok 5: 6 Trillion Parameters, 7% Odds, and a Lot of Spin
videos.summary_source_text:
Grok 5: 6 Trillion Parameters, 7% Odds, and a Lot of Spin Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Elon Musk says Grok 5 has a 10% chance of being "indistinguishable from AGI." The Polymarket crowd gives it a 7% chance of even shipping by June 30. So what's actually going on?

I spent six months tracking every Grok 5 leak, spec sheet, and investor comment to separate what's confirmed from what's spin. The only official statement from xAI is one sentence: "Grok 5 is currently in training." Everything else — the six trillion parameters, the 1.5 million token context window, the Colossus 2 supercomputer — comes from leaks and tweets, not benchmarks.

In this video we cover the realistic specs, the slipping timeline, the gigawatt-scale infrastructure nobody's talking about, the real deployment deals (the Pentagon, El Salvador, Tesla, Optimus), and how Grok 5 actually stacks up against GPT-5, Claude, Llama, and Gemini.

No hype. No fake benchmarks. Just what the evidence actually supports.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 — Intro: The hype vs. the release dates
00:37 — What Grok actually is right now
02:18 — The release date nobody wants to say out loud
03:48 — What the specs probably look like
06:28 — The infrastructure story that actually matters
08:02 — Benchmarks and the reality check
10:02 — Where Grok 5 actually gets used
12:05 — How it stacks up against everyone else
13:49 — The risks nobody wants to talk about
15:13 — What to actually watch for

💬 Drop your prediction in the comments: does Grok 5 ship in Q3, or slip into 2027?

🔔 Subscribe for a no-hype breakdown of every major AI model release. Next up: what GPT-6 is shaping up to look like.

videos.summary_source_text:
Simple Hip Drill for Solid Contact #shorts #golf #golfer #golfswing #ericcogorno #shorts 

In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno reveals the most overlooked fundamental in the golf swing – hips moving foward during the downswing. Eric Cogorno demonstrates how moving your hips in golf swing forward by 4-6 inches creates two essential benefits: superior low point control for ball-first contact and an inside swing path for straighter shots. Through detailed analysis of PGA Tour Pro swings and practical golf drills, you'll master this non-negotiable movement that transforms inconsistent ball strikers into solid players virtually overnight.

What You'll Learn:

• How to use your hips in the golf swing to move 4-6 inches forward for solid contact
• Essential low point control techniques that ensure ball-first, ground-second impact
• Golf drills using alignment sticks to practice proper hip movement in the golf swing
• PGA Tour Pro hip movement patterns from Scotty Scheffler, Tommy Fleetwood, and Rory McIlroy
• Two proven feelings for initiating forward hip drive during your golf swing
• Proper setup positions that make forward hip movement easier and more natural
